title: agrostis stolonifera f. humilis
intro: Agrostis stolonifera f. humilis, also known as creeping bentgrass or bent grass, is a subspecies of grass that grows in dense mats or tufts, typically found in wetlands, marshes, and other areas with moist soils. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties, which can help soothe skin irritations, wounds, and fungal infections. The health benefits of Agrostis stolonifera f. humilis include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which can be used to treat various health issues, although more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal properties and potential interactions.
